summary: The Cleanup artifacts job failed when attempting to delete artifacts; the gh CLI returned HTTP 403 'Resource not accessible by integration', causing the step to exit with code 1.
root_cause: The gh CLI call to delete artifacts for run ID 23485708914 returned "Resource not accessible by integration (HTTP 403)", indicating the Actions workflow's token or permissions do not allow deleting artifacts.

evidence.reason: Shows the step that attempted to delete artifacts and the target run ID.

evidence.quote: gh: Resource not accessible by integration (HTTP 403)
evidence.reason: Direct error from the gh CLI indicating an authorization/permission denial.
Shows the job failed as a result of the error returned by the gh command.

suggested_fix: Ensure the workflow runs with a token that has permission to manage artifacts (for example set workflow permissions to 'actions: write' or use a PAT with appropriate scopes) and confirm the 'gh' command is using that token.

summary: The 'Cleanup artifacts' job failed while deleting artifacts because the gh CLI returned a 403 'Resource not accessible by integration', causing the step to exit with code 1.
root_cause: The GitHub CLI call to delete artifacts failed with "Resource not accessible by integration (HTTP 403)", indicating the token/integration used by the job lacks permission to access or delete the artifacts.

evidence.reason: Shows the job was attempting to delete artifacts when the failure occurred.

evidence.quote: gh: Resource not accessible by integration (HTTP 403)
evidence.reason: Direct error message from the gh CLI indicating an authorization/permission issue (HTTP 403).
Shows the job failed as a result of the gh CLI error.

suggested_fix: Give the integration/token used by the 'Cleanup artifacts' job permission to access and delete artifacts. For example, update the workflow to grant the needed permissions to GITHUB_TOKEN (or provide a PAT with artifact/delete scopes), or adjust the 'gh' authentication so the CLI can delete artifacts, then re-run the job.

summary: The docs status-check job exited with code 1 because a shell conditional did not match the status string and executed the else branch that calls exit 1.
root_cause: A shell if-condition in the workflow compared the string "failure" only against "success" and "skipped", so the condition was false and the else branch ran 'exit 1', causing the job to fail.

evidence.reason: Shows the conditional is comparing the literal string "failure" only to "success" and "skipped", which will be false.
Shows the else branch explicitly calls exit 1, which will fail the job when the condition is false.
Confirms the job terminated with an exit code 1 as a result of the script's exit.

suggested_fix: Update the workflow's shell conditional to handle the actual status value (for example, include "failure" in the allowed comparisons or change the logic so it doesn't call 'exit 1' for this case), e.g. adjust the if expression so it does not fall through to the 'exit 1' else branch.

summary: The CI 'Run prettier' step failed because the yarn-run command exited with a non-zero status during the formatting step.
root_cause: A yarn-run command invoked by the formatting job exited with code 1, causing the Prettier step to fail.

evidence.quote: error Command failed with exit code 1.
evidence.reason: Shows the yarn-run command used by the job terminated with a non-zero exit code, which caused the job to fail.

evidence.quote: info Visit https://yarnpkg.com/en/docs/cli/run for documentation about this command.
evidence.reason: Indicates the failing command was run via yarn, consistent with running a project script such as a prettier check.
A repository file listed in the job output; such files are typical targets for formatting checks like Prettier.
Another repository file listed in the output that may have been checked by the formatting step.

suggested_fix: Run the project's prettier script locally (e.g. via the same yarn script used in CI) to identify and fix formatting issues; then commit the formatted files. Example: run the CI's prettier command locally or run 'yarn <prettier-script-name> --write' (or the repository's documented prettier command) and push the changes.

summary: The CI failed because a doctest in the documentation (doc/callbacks.rst) failed, causing the pytest job to report one failing test.
root_cause: A doctest in /home/runner/work/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/doc/callbacks.rst at line 48 raised a DocTestFailure, causing the test run to fail.

evidence.reason: Indicates that the test run encountered failures.
Shows the exact file, line, and that a DocTestFailure occurred.
Identifies the failing doctest test identifier.
Confirms the test summary showing one failing test among the doc tests.

suggested_fix: Open doc/callbacks.rst and inspect the doctest at line 48; update the example or its expected output so it matches actual behavior, then re-run the doctests (e.g. pytest ../doc/callbacks.rst::callbacks.rst) to verify the fix.

summary: A doctest in the documentation failed: the doctest in doc/callbacks.rst produced a DocTestFailure which caused the CI job to fail despite many other tests passing.
root_cause: DocTestFailure in /home/runner/work/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/doc/callbacks.rst at line 48: the doctest output does not match the expected output.

evidence.reason: Shows the exact file and line where a doctest failed (DocTestFailure).
Indicates that the callbacks.rst doctest failed during the test run.
Marks that there was at least one test failure in the test suite.
Summary line showing there was exactly 1 failed test among the test results.

suggested_fix: Inspect doc/callbacks.rst around line 48 and update the doctest so its expected output matches the actual output (or update the example code). Then re-run the doctest (e.g., pytest ../doc/callbacks.rst::callbacks.rst) to confirm the fix.
